content_intent: Explain how Azure Functions triggers work and their key types

top_3_suggestions.why_this_fits: The content presents 4 distinct trigger types as parallel concepts with no procedural dependency.
A mindmap groups the trigger types around the central Azure Functions concept.
Iceberg can show visible trigger invocation above the line and hidden runtime context below.

diagram_generation_inputs.visual_query: Show the 4 key trigger types for Azure Functions
content: HTTP Trigger – invoke a function via HTTP request
Timer Trigger – run on a schedule using CRON expressions
Blob Trigger – react to new files in Azure Blob Storage
Queue Trigger – process messages from Azure Storage Queue
diagram_generation_inputs.context_notes: Azure Functions serverless compute intro video. Audience: developers new to serverless.

notes_for_human_review: Rank 1 (Key Ideas) is the safest choice for an intro video — clear and readable.
Rank 3 (Iceberg) only works well if the narration explicitly contrasts visible vs hidden complexity.
Consider using Rank 2 (Mindmap) if the video segment is longer and needs more visual richness.
